steps.title: Find $y$
steps.content: So $y=3$. That matches choice D — D is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice D is correct. Dividing both sides of the equation $10x=110$ by $10$ yields $x=11$. Substituting $11$ for $x$ in the equation $6x-63=y$ yields $6(11)-63=y$, which is equivalent to $66-63=y$, or $3=y$. Therefore, the value of $y$ is $3$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is incorrect. This is the value of $x$, not $y$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice C is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.
